SEARCH
Package org.apache.arrow.datafusion.execution

Class SessionConfig

java.lang.Object
org.apache.arrow.datafusion.execution.SessionConfig
All Implemented Interfaces:
AutoCloseable
public class SessionConfig extends Object implements AutoCloseable
Snapshot of a DataFusion session's configuration.

Returned by TaskContext.sessionConfig() and accepted by TaskContext.withSessionConfig(SessionConfig). The Rust type wraps ConfigOptions plus session-specific extras (query planner, expression planners, etc.); this Java binding currently exposes only the flat options map via options(). Use fromConfigOptions(ConfigOptions) to build one from an existing ConfigOptions.

Example: 

SessionConfig cfg = taskContext.sessionConfig();
String batchSize = cfg.options().get("datafusion.execution.batch_size");
See Also:
  • Rust DataFusion: SessionConfig

  • Method Details

    • fromConfigOptions

      public static SessionConfig fromConfigOptions(ConfigOptions options)
      Creates a SessionConfig from a Java-side ConfigOptions. The options are flattened to key/value strings and sent across FFI.

      Example: 

      SessionConfig cfg = SessionConfig.fromConfigOptions(
    ConfigOptions.fromStringMap(Map.of("datafusion.execution.batch_size", "4096")));
      Parameters:
      options - the source config
      Returns:
      a new SessionConfig
      See Also:
      • Rust DataFusion: SessionConfig::from_string_hash_map

    • options

      public Map<String,String> options()
      Returns the configured options as a flat map.

      Example: 

      Map<String, String> opts = sessionConfig.options();
      Returns:
      the options map
      See Also:
      • Rust DataFusion: SessionConfig::options

    • close

      public void close()
      Specified by:
      close in interface AutoCloseable
